summary: Soundboard source with noticeable speed‑up and dropouts, yet a scorching second set highlighted by a monster Eyes of the World and fire‑filled Samson & Delilah.
review: The show is celebrated for its incendiary second half – Brent’s keys blaze on Samson & Delilah, the band delivers an epic Eyes of the World, and Let It Grow erupts as a monster.  Recording-wise the SBD suffers from fast‑playback quirks and occasional drops/cuts that detract from the listening experience, though the performance itself remains highly praised.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (especially the second set) giving it ~4‑5 stars, but many note speed and dropout problems that lower the technical rating to ~2‑3 stars; weighted average yields a mid‑range score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers report fast playback (OGG/other formats) and audible drops/cuts; no consistent mention of crispness, indicating the source is usable but flawed.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as average or merely solid, while the second set is repeatedly called fire‑hot, with the band building momentum from Samson into a soaring Eyes of the World and a monster Let It Grow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for energetic playing, dancing during Samson & Delilah, creative MIDI work on Lovelight, and overall “traversing the planet” vibe.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned as above‑average on Tom Thumb's; otherwise no specific comments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted having fun with Tom Thumb lyrics and dancing on Samson, though one reviewer jokes about a ‘Bob asthma attack’, indicating mixed but generally positive remarks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keys) described as “on fire” for Samson & Delilah and delivering strong support throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum performances highlighted as among the best of the era, especially during the extended jam sections.
